meta.name: Sub-3:30 Marathon
meta.subtitle: Good for age territory
meta.description: Sub-3:30 (4:58/km) puts you in the top 20% of marathon finishers. This plan requires consistent 75+ km weeks at its peak.

weeks.title: Base Building — Week 1
weeks.note: Week 1: Build your aerobic base. Every run should feel comfortable — you're conditioning your body to handle more.

sessions.det: 11km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
6km very easy · 6:30–7:00/km · Shake out the legs. Slower than you think you need.
11km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
11km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
21km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

weeks.title: Build Phase — Week 8
weeks.note: Week 8: Quality over quantity. The interval and tempo sessions are where fitness is made. Protect your sleep this week.

sessions.det: 8km tempo · 4:45–5:05/km · Comfortably hard. You can speak in short sentences only.
6km very easy · 6:30–7:00/km · Shake out the legs. Slower than you think you need.
10km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
7km easy then 4 × 100m strides · Start slow, build to 90% in the final 30m of each stride.
16km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.

weeks.title: Peak Training — Week 16
weeks.note: Week 16: Peak training week. This is hard — it's meant to be. Trust the process and execute each session.

sessions.det: 13km tempo · 4:45–5:05/km · Comfortably hard. You can speak in short sentences only.
9km very easy · 6:30–7:00/km · Shake out the legs. Slower than you think you need.
10km total · 400m reps at 5K race pace · Full recovery between reps. Quality over quantity.
15km easy · 5:50–6:20/km · Conversational pace. If you can't hold a conversation, you're going too fast.
26km long · 6:00–6:30/km · Easy, conversational effort throughout. The longest run of your week.
